cmad.notes: No SOHO-specific CMAD located. SOHO is a legacy mission (launched 1995-12-02) predating the CMAD requirement. A web search for 'SOHO Calibration and Measurement Algorithms Document' returned CMADs only for other missions (STEREO/SECCHI, STEREO/WAVES, MMS). A related radiometric-calibration reference exists (ISSI 'The Radiometric Calibration of SOHO', https://www.issibern.ch/PDF-Files/soho_cal.pdf) but it is not a CMAD front-page team document. Proceeded with record Contacts, instrument SPASE records, and the SOHO provider people page for current-team evidence.

evidence.source: SOHO mission overview paper co-author (Domingo, Fleck & Poland 1995)

enrichment.notes: ORCID 0000-0001-5777-9121 confirmed via its works (e.g. 'SOHO's 30-year legacy of observing the Sun', helioseismology) consistent with the SOHO Project Scientist. AFFILIATION DISCREPANCY flagged for reviewer: ORCID employment lists 'National Aeronautics and Space Administration' (undated) while the finder value is 'European Space Agency'. Fleck is an ESA scientist resident at NASA GSFC, so both are defensible; retained the finder's ESA value.

evidence.source: SOHO provider people page (Deputy Project Scientist)

enrichment.notes: Pre-filled ORCID: ISO 7064 checksum valid; corroborated by ORCID employment 'Goddard Space Flight Center' (Greenbelt MD, ROR 0171mag52), consistent with the NASA GSFC Deputy Project Scientist. Employment undated -> labeled current; agrees with finder affiliation.

Pre-filled ORCID: checksum valid; corroborated by ORCID works (WISPR/PSP, SoloHI, coronagraph imaging) consistent with the NRL solar-coronagraph PI lineage (LASCO/SECCHI/WISPR). ORCID has no employment entries. AFFILIATION flagged: retained finder's 'US Naval Research Laboratory'; finder noted the SMWG Person record lists Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory instead - discrepancy unresolved, for reviewer.

evidence.source: SOHO provider people page (current LASCO PI)

enrichment.notes: Pre-filled ORCID: checksum valid; corroborated by ORCID employment 'US Naval Research Laboratory' (Space Science Division, Heliospheric Physics Branch), 2008-10 -> present, which overlaps the SOHO operating span (1995-present) -> era-matched. Agrees with finder affiliation; consistent with current LASCO PI.

ORCID employment 'Stanford University' (Professor), 1986 -> present, overlaps the operating span -> era-matched and matches the MDI PI institution. Confirms identity; agrees with finder affiliation.

Agrees with finder affiliation.

As current UVCS PI his NRL affiliation is retained.

Single 'Claus Fröhlich' match; corroborated by works on total-solar-irradiance (TSI) composite construction, matching the VIRGO original PI. Recorded for downstream awareness as a potential FormerProjectScientist.

evidence.source: Lead author, SOHO mission overview paper (Domingo, Fleck & Poland 1995, Solar Physics 162, 1)

notes: The Observatory record itself lists only two Contacts (both ProjectScientist: Jack Ireland and Bernhard Fleck). The Observatory XML carries no InstrumentIDs and no OperatingSpan; the SOHO instrument records were found via the SMWG Instrument registry (https://spase-metadata.org/SMWG/Instrument/SOHO/) and corroborated by the SOHO provider people page.

No SOHO-specific CMAD exists — see the top-level cmad field. SOHO (launched 1995) predates the CMAD requirement; current-team evidence came instead from the provider people page and instrument SPASE records.

SOHO has two distinct PI generations. The instrument SPASE records carry the ORIGINAL (launch-era, 1995) PIs, while the provider people page (https://soho.nascom.nasa.gov/about/people.html) lists the CURRENT PIs. Where they differ, BOTH are included as Strong (each is a named instrument PI from an authoritative source): EIT Delaboudiniere/Auchere, CDS Harrison/Fludra, GOLF Gabriel/Boumier, SUMER Wilhelm/Curdt, SWAN Bertaux/Quemerais, UVCS Kohl+Culhane/Strachan, LASCO Howard/Battams, VIRGO Frohlich/Finsterle. The downstream role agent should resolve original-vs-current (e.g. FormerPI vs PI) and activity dates.
MDI, CELIAS, COSTEP, ERNE show the same person in both the SPASE record and the provider page (Scherrer, Wimmer-Schweingruber, Heber, Vainio).

Provider people page reads roles as: Fleck = Project Scientist, Ireland = Deputy Project Scientist, Schenk = Project Manager. The SPASE record currently labels both Fleck and Ireland as ProjectScientist; downstream may wish to reconcile Ireland to Deputy Project Scientist.

The SOHO mission overview paper (Domingo, Fleck & Poland 1995, Solar Physics 162, 1; doi:10.1007/BF00733425) corroborates Fleck and names Vicente Domingo (first SOHO Project Scientist) and Arthur I. Poland as overview-paper authors/early project scientists. Both were excluded (overview co-authorship alone is below the inclusion bar and neither appears in the record Contacts nor the current provider listing) but recorded for downstream awareness as potential FormerProjectScientist candidates.
Person record for Kevin Schenk does not exist (404); the 21 included instrument/project PIs have existing SMWG Person records as cited.
Affiliation for Russell A. Howard differs between sources (provider page: US Naval Research Laboratory for LASCO; SMWG Person record lists Johns Hopkins APL) — flagged for downstream verification.

operating_span.notes: SOHO launched 1995-12-02 and is still operating; open-ended span used for era-matching. The Observatory SPASE record carries no explicit OperatingSpan (per finder notes).
